news.title: NCLT allows withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against Nobility Estates
news.para: The NCLT has allowed withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against Nobility Estates, developer of ATS Group’s Le Grandiose project in Noida, after lenders and the erstwhile management reached a settlement. The tribunal noted that the withdrawal proposal received 92.52% approval from the committee of creditors, including votes from 636 homebuyers, and directed restoration of management control to the company’s board.

news.title: India's USD 1.5-billion proptech market is booming - but almost no one is making money
news.para: A deep-dive brokerage report reveals a sector caught in a structural bind: residential real estate transactions have hit record highs, yet the digital platforms riding that boom, from listing portals to transaction brokers to home interior firms, are burning cash, losing the advertising war to Google and Meta, and scrambling to reinvent themselves by piling into each other's businesses.

news.title: Berger Paints' net profit declines 4.63% to ₹1,128.02 crore in FY26
news.para: Berger Paints saw its net profit dip by 4.63% in FY26. The company's total income grew by 2.99%. In the fourth quarter, profit after tax surged by 27.52%. Price increases are expected to support margins. The board recommended a dividend of ₹4 per share.

news.title: Welspun One leases 65,000 sq ft at JNPA SEZ to Balmer Lawrie
news.para: Welspun One has entered into a letter of intent with Balmer Lawrie & Co. for 65,000 sq ft of grade A warehousing space at WTC Nhava Sheva, expected to be operational by early 2027. This strategic move will enhance Balmer Lawrie's existing logistics operations within the JNPA SEZ, offering dedicated SEZ warehousing and value-added services to customers.

news.title: Keystone Realtors' net profit declines 30.81% to ₹94.95 crore in FY26
news.para: Keystone Realtors saw its net profit fall by over 30% in FY26. However, its total income increased by nearly 28%. The company achieved strong pre-sales growth and expanded its market share. Collections also saw a significant rise. Keystone Realtors completed six projects during the financial year.

news.title: Government to auction 12 limestone blocks in Jammu & Kashmir
news.para: The government is set to auction 12 limestone blocks in Jammu & Kashmir on May 12, 2026, in Srinagar. This initiative aims to revive the UT's mining sector and boost the local economy by offering blocks with promising geological potential. The auction reflects a commitment to resource utilization and sustained industry participation.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad: PCMC snaps water supply to 24 housing societies over non-functional STPs
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad Municipal Corporation has cut water supply to 24 housing societies for non-functional sewage treatment plants and fined 16 complexes for discharging wastewater. The civic body warned of further action against defaulting societies, emphasizing the mandatory installation and operation of STPs for complexes with over 100 flats.

news.title: Lucknow development body to launch housing project with 900 LIG, EWS flats on Sitapur Road
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority is set to launch a new affordable housing project on Sitapur Road. Approximately 900 flats will be developed for Low Income Group and Economically Weaker Section families. The project will feature 2BHK and 1BHK units. Ample parking and green spaces are also planned. Tendering for construction is expected to commence soon.

news.title: Brookfield India REIT's net operating income rises 23.8% in FY26
news.para: Brookfield India REIT reported a significant 53% year-on-year growth in net operating income to ₹742.9 crore for Q4 FY26. The company declared a distribution of ₹5.50 per unit for the quarter, contributing to a full-year distribution of ₹21.40 per unit. Strong leasing and the acquisition of Ecoworld bolstered performance, with net consolidated profit rising 30.81% for FY26.

news.title: Maharashtra to digitise registration records from 1865; nearly 30 crore pages to go online
news.para: Maharashtra's Registration and Stamps Department is digitizing over a century of land records, aiming to convert 30 crore pages from 1865 onwards into a searchable digital format. This ambitious project will enhance property transparency, streamline transactions, and ensure long-term preservation of historical documents, making them accessible online for citizens.

news.title: Over 1,000 homes flout stilt parking norms in Gurugram, DLF phases top list
news.para: Gurugram authorities have launched a major crackdown on illegal use of stilt parking areas in residential buildings. Thousands of properties across several colonies show violations, with stilt floors converted into rooms and commercial spaces. This misuse exacerbates parking shortages and traffic congestion.

news.title: Ludhiana’s property plates project stuck in 12-year digital limbo
news.para: Ludhiana's decade-long property modernization effort is stalled after two failed attempts to install unique identification plates. A Smart City Mission project collapsed due to contractor disputes and a shift from GIS to drone surveys, leaving only 85,000 plates installed. Officials now deem a resurvey necessary to update the property database before the initiative can resume.

news.title: New Gujarat policy may give freehold status to housing board colonies
news.para: Gujarat government is preparing a new housing policy. This policy aims to boost affordable housing by redeveloping old housing board colonies. Rules will be relaxed, and colonies may get freehold status. This will allow easier sale and transfer of properties. Developers will receive incentives like higher floor space index. The goal is to create cost-effective homes in prime locations.

news.title: Noida: Residents of Great Value Sharanam flag structural safety, poor infrastructure
news.para: Residents of Great Value Sharanam in Noida Sector 107 are concerned about the deteriorating condition of their housing complex. Issues include poor waste management, inadequate safety systems, and widespread seepage. A structural audit revealed significant risks in many building columns. While some repair work has begun, residents are dissatisfied with the slow progress and lack of a completion timeline.

news.title: Civic body proposes township over 69 acres in Airoli, Navi Mumbai
news.para: Cidco is developing a massive 69-acre residential township in Airoli, with a developer selected based on the highest revenue share. This project, spanning two decades, aims to meet growing housing demand and boost the node's infrastructure.  The initiative, approved by the state government, is expected to significantly enhance Airoli's property market and connectivity.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh asks Noida DM to act against Migsun Builders over illegal groundwater use
news.para: The groundwater department has directed the Noida DM to act against Migsun Builders for excessive groundwater extraction in Greater Noida. Environmentalist Vikrant Tongad alleged the builder is discharging extracted groundwater into the sewer system, violating the UP Groundwater Act and causing a decline in water levels. Residents and farmers are distressed by the illegal extraction and wastage.

news.title: War puts brakes on UAE realty boom as Dubai projects face delays
news.para: The current turmoil in West Asia is having a notable effect on the property market in the UAE. Developers are experiencing significant delays in delivering new homes due to ongoing supply chain challenges and soaring costs.

news.title: Australia set for bold housing tax overhaul, budget deficit to narrow
news.para: Australia is set for a leaner budget deficit this year, thanks to surging revenue from its booming commodities sector. In a move poised to shake up the housing market, the government plans to introduce significant changes to property taxes, which could alter the landscape of capital gains tax discounts and the practice of negative gearing.

news.title: Delhi government tightens dust control norms, makes 100 GSM green nets mandatory at C&D sites
news.para: Delhi mandates high-density dust screens of at least 100 GSM at all construction and demolition sites to combat air pollution. This move, following CAQM guidelines, aims to effectively contain PM2.5 and PM10 at the source. The government is also enhancing its 'Dust Portal 2.0' for better monitoring and enforcement.

news.title: CBI files seventh chargesheet in builder-banker nexus 'scam' against Sequel Buildcon
news.para: The CBI has filed its seventh chargesheet in a major builder-banker scam. Sequel Buildcon Private Limited is accused of cheating homebuyers and misusing funds from PNB Housing Finance. The company allegedly failed to deliver residential units or refund payments. This case is part of a larger investigation into 50 similar matters handed over by the Supreme Court.
